name: Lemon Balm
summary: Lemon balm may support gentle calm, stress-related sleep, and relaxed focus without heavy sedation.
description: Gentle calming support for stress/sleep/focus contexts.
safetyNotes: No serious side effects in reviewed anxiety/depression trials, but sedation and thyroid/medication flags should be reviewed before strong recommendations.

sources.title: Depression and Its Phytopharmacotherapy-A Narrative Review

sources.title: The effects of lemon balm (Melissa officinalis L.) on depression and anxiety in clinical trials: A systematic review and meta-analysis
sources.authors: Javid Ghazizadeh; S. Sadigh-Eteghad; W. Marx; A. Fakhari; S. Hamedeyazdan; Mohammadali Torbati; Somaiyeh Taheri-Tarighi; Mostafa Araj-khodaei; M. Mirghafourvand
sources.note: lemon balm anxiety/depression evidence and heterogeneity caution

claimMap.claim: Lemon balm may improve anxiety and depressive symptom scores, particularly acutely, but evidence is heterogeneous.
claimMap.notes: Lemon balm may improve anxiety and depressive symptom scores, particularly acutely, but evidence is heterogeneous.

claimMap.claim: v9.5: Mapped to lemon balm evidence; keep claims mild and anxiety-focused.
claimMap.notes: v9.5: Mapped to lemon balm evidence; keep claims mild and anxiety-focused.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Lemon balm dose varies by extract; clinical trials commonly use standardized Melissa officinalis preparations rather than loose tea

dosage: Varies by extract/form; newer standardized extracts should be tracked separately from tea, essential oil, or combination products. | Lemon balm extracts, oral formulations, and sometimes aromatherapy/combination products; do not merge.
typical_dosage: Varies by extract/form; newer standardized extracts should be tracked separately from tea, essential oil, or combination products. | Lemon balm extracts, oral formulations, and sometimes aromatherapy/combination products; do not merge.
